name: Apple Raspberry Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients for the crust: flour, lard, vinegar, salt, and cold water. Mix until a dough forms.
2. Place the dough on a lightly floured surface and roll out to a rectangle about 8 X 12 inches.
3. Fold the dough into thirds and refrigerate until ready to use.
4. Peel and core 8 large Granny Smith apples, then dice them into eighth-sized pieces.
5. Cut each apple piece in half crosswise, creating 1 1/2 inch chunks.
6. Toss the diced apples with lemon juice.
7. In a separate bowl, mix sugar, flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves.
8. Add the spice mixture to the apples and toss until evenly coated.
9. Gently fold in the raspberries and toss lightly.
10. Reserve 1/3 of the dough for rolling out later.
11. Roll out the remaining dough into a round about 5 inches larger than your pie dish.
12. Place the rolled dough into the prepared dish and trim any excess overhang to about 1 inch.
13. Pour the apple mixture into the crust, spreading it evenly.
14. Roll out the remaining dough and place it on top of the apples. Crimp the edges.
15. Sprinkle the remaining sugar over the top crust.
16. Cut 5 slits in the center of the dough to allow steam to escape during baking.
17. Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 10 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 400°F (200°C) and bake for an additional 40 minutes or until golden brown and set. Allow to cool before serving.
